title: rubus infestus var. drejeri
intro: Rubus infestus var. drejeri, also known as the Japanese raspberry or thimbleberry, is a type of thimbleberry that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus infestus var. drejeri include its potential to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and aid in the treatment of various digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Rubus infestus var. drejeri can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world.
